revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天之都度閇知泥神]]



{{infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| children = {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}
| father = 
| consort = 
| mother = 
| spouse = {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}
| gender = {{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}
| offspring = {{ill|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|1=Omizunu}}
}}[[Ame-no-Tsudoechine]] was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}}.<ref name="fountainhead">{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21|qq=}}</ref>{{rp|278|qq=}}
Her ancestry is unclear but she married {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}} and became an ancestress of [[Ōkuninushi]]<ref name="fountainhead" />{{rp|278|qq=}}
==Overview==
{{Infobox Japanese deity
| name = Amatsu-Tsunodopechinokami
| generation = 
| predecessor = 
| successor = 
| classification = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}
| full_name = Amatsu-Tsunodopechinokami (天之都度閇知泥神)
| alternate_names = Amatsu-Tsunodohechinokami, Amano-Tsunode-no-Chine
| epithet = 
| Rank = 
| domain = {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|qid=Q3137449|lt=water god|1=Suijin}}
| shrine = 
| father = 
| mother = 
| relatives = 
| spouse = {{ill|Fukafuchi-no-Mizuya-Relahana-no-Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi-no-Mizuya-Relahana-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}
| children = {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}
| texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}
}}
'''Amatsu-Tsunodopechinokami''' (天之都度閇知泥神) is a goddess appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview ==
In the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}*, she is described as the wife of {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}. She does not appear in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*. In the records of {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|Awaga Shrine|qid=Q11604651|lt=Awaga Shrine|1=Awaga Shrine}} titled *{{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}}*, she is identified as '''Amano-Tsunode-no-Chine''' (阿麻乃都刀閇乃知尼), the wife of {{ill|Funabonomochikunusunokami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabonomochikunusunokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}}, and is recorded as the mother of {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}.
The etymology of her name is considered unclear. However, "天之" (*Amatsu*, "heavenly") is thought not to signify {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} but rather to indicate a water source. "都度閇" (*Tsunodope*) is interpreted as "gather" (*tsudoe*), suggesting being gathered by invisible divine power. "知" (*chi*) is interpreted as "path" or "waterway," while "泥" (*ne*) is an honorific. Together, the name is taken to mean "the heavenly waterway gathered above"<ref>Kokugakuin University Kojiki Center</ref>.
== Genealogy ==
[[File:Susanowo family tree.png|thumb|250px|Genealogy from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} to {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (based on the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}*). Blue indicates male deities, red indicates female deities.]]
Amatsu-Tsunodopechinokami is the wife of {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}, who is the child of {{ill|Funabonomochikunusunokami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funabonomochikunusunokami|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}} and {{ill|Hikawashime|ja|日河比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawashime|1=Hikawahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65270244}}. Together, they have a child, {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}.

{{Reflist|qq=}}
